pi_items.source: Downloaded and inspected https://imagej.net/ij/images/embryos.jpg with PIL Image
Actual embryos.jpg width is 1600 pixels; side-by-side montage would be 3200 pixels
Applied CLAHE to grayscale embryos image using scikit-image.exposure.equalize_adapthist; original entropy=4.4663, enhanced=6.6296, gain=2.1633 (48.44% increase)
Confirmed in export_result.sh and setup_task.sh scripts; directory creation verified in setup_imagej.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real ImageJ built-in sample image 'embryos.jpg' (1600×1200 pixels, 128.9 KB) accessed via File > Open Samples > Embryos in Fiji/ImageJ. The image designation '(42K)' in metadata is incorrect; the actual file is 128.9 KB. The task applies CLAHE (Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization) to create a duplicate, which demonstrably increases image entropy from 4.47 to 6.63 (gain of 2.16, far exceeding the minimum required 0.05). A side-by-side montage of original and enhanced images would have dimensions approximately 3200×1200 pixels, both dimensions exceeding the stated minimums (500×200). The output PNG is saved to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/clahe_comparison.png, with anti-gaming measures (timestamp checking) implemented in the task scripts.
